*discovery.EndpointSlice) {\\n172: \\tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\\n173: \\tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\\n174: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\\n175: \\t\\tcurrentDiff \\t\\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\\n197: \\t}\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\\n201: // nil\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\\n217: \\tupdated := false\\n218: \\tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n219: \\tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n220: \\n221: \\t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\\n222: \\t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\\n223: \\tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\\n224: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n225: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n226: \\t\\t}\\n227: \\t\\t// copy endpoint slice labels\\n228: \\t\\tepLabels[key] = value\\n229: \\t}\\n230: \\n231: \\tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\\n232: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n233: \\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Service using reserved endpoint slices label\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"skipping\\\", key, \\\"label\\\", value)\\n234: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n235: \\t\\t}\\n236: \\t\\t// copy service labels\\n237: \\t\\tsvcLabels[key] = value\\n238: \\t}\\n239: \\n240: \\t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the the service Type\\n246: \\tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\\n247: \\t\\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \\\"\\\"\\n248: \\t} else {\\n249: \\t\\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\\n250: \\t}\\n251: \\n252: \\t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\\n253: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\\n254: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\\n255: \\n256: \\treturn svcLabels, updated\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\\n261: \\tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\\n262: \\t\\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\\n263: \\t\\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\\n264: \\t\\treturn true\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\treturn false\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\\n270: // endpoints they contain.\\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\\n272: \\n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\\n276: \\treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\\n277: }\\n278: \\n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\\n281: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\\n282: \\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\\n283: \\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\\n284: \\t// v1.IPFamily types\\n285: \\n286: \\t// // we have found families for this service\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\\n303: \\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\\n304: \\n305: \\t// Why do we need this:\\n306: \\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\\n307: \\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\\n308: \\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\\n309: \\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\\n310: \\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\\n311: \\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\\n312: \\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\\n313: \\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\\n314: \\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\\n315: \\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\\n316: \\n317: \\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\\n318: \\t\\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\\n319: \\t\\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\\n320: \\t\\t\\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\\n323: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"addressType\\\", addrType, \\\"clusterIP\\\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\\n324: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\t// headless\\n328: \\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\\n329: \\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\\n330: \\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\\n331: \\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\\n332: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n333: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n334: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service))\\n335: \\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n339: \\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\\n340: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n341: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\\n344: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n347: \\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\\n348: \\t\\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\\n349: \\t\\t\\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\treturn unchangedSlices\\n354: }\\n355: \\n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\\n358: // with a value set to \\\"Auto\\\" or \\\"auto\\\". \\t\\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\t}\\n395: \\tcase intstr.Int:\\n396: \\t\\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\\\", nil\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, service\\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\\n281: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\\n282: \\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\\n283: \\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\\n284: \\t// v1.IPFamily types\\n285: \\n286: \\t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\\n287: \\t// as it gets deprecated\\n288: \\tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\\n289: \\t\\tif family == \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\\n299: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\\n303: \\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\\n304: \\n305: \\t// Why do we need this:\\n306: \\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\\n307: \\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\\n308: \\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\\n309: \\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\\n310: \\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\\n311: \\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\\n312: \\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\\n313: \\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\\n314: \\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\\n315: \\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\\n316: \\n317: \\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\\n318: \\t\\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\\n319: \\t\\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\\n320: \\t\\t\\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\\n323: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"addressType\\\", addrType, \\\"clusterIP\\\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\\n324: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\t// headless\\n328: \\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\\n329: \\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\\n330: \\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\\n331: \\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\\n332: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n333: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n334: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service))\\n335: \\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n339: \\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\\n340: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n341: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\\n344: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n347: \\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\\n348: \\t\\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\\n349: \\t\\t\\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\treturn unchangedSlices\\n354: }\\n355: \\n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\\n358: // with a value set to \\\"Auto\\\" or \\\"auto\\\". When both are set,\\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\\n361: \\tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\\n362: \\tif !ok {\\n363: \\t\\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\\n364: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n366: \\t\\t}\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn val == \\\"Auto\\\" || val == \\\"auto\\\"\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\\n375: \\treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \\\"\\\"\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\\n379: // targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\\n380: // string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\\n381: // match is found, fail.\\n382: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n383: func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\\n384: \\tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\\n385: \\tswitch portName.Type {\\n386: \\tcase intstr.String:\\n387: \\t\\tname := portName.StrVal\\n388: \\t\\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n389: \\t\\t\\tfor \\t\\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\t}\\n395: \\tcase intstr.Int:\\n396: \\t\\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\\\", pod.UID)\\n400: }\\n401: and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := ports.\n80: \tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\n81: \t\treturn endpointPorts\n82: \t}\n83: \n84: \tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\n85: {\n112: \t\tisIPv6PodIP := utilnet.IsIPv6String(podIP.IP)\n113: \t\tif isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv6 {\n114: \t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n115: \t\t}\n116: // newEndpointSlice returns an EndpointSlice generated from a service and\n126: // endpointMeta.\n127: func newEndpointSlice(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, endpointMeta *endpointMeta, controllerName string) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n128: \tgvk := schema.GroupVersionKind{Version: \"v1\", Kind: \"Service\"}\n129: \townerRef := metav1.NewControllerRef(service, gvk)\n130: \tepSlice EndpointSlice is owned by the provided\n158: // Service.\n159: func ownedBy(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n160: \tfor _, o := range endpointSlice.OwnerReferences {\n161: \t\tif o.UID == svc.UID && o.Kind == \"Service\" && o.APIVersion == \"v1\" {\n162: \t\t\treturn true\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t}\n165: \treturn false\n166: }\n167: \n168: // getSliceToFill will return the EndpointSlice that will be closest to full\n169: // when numEndpoints are added. If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\n170: // will be returned.\n171: func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n172: \tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\n173: \tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\n174: \tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n175: \t\tcurrentDiff := maxEndpoints - (numEndpoints + len(endpointSlice.Endpoints))\n176: \t\tif currentDiff >= 0 && currentDiff < closestDiff {\n177: \t\t\tclosestDiff = currentDiff\n178: \t\t\tclosestSlice = endpointSlice\n179: \t\t\tif closestDiff == 0 {\n180: \t\t\t\treturn closestSlice\n181: \t\t\t}\n182: \t\t}\n183: \t}\n184: \treturn closestSlice\n185: }\n186: \n187: // addTriggerTimeAnnotation adds a triggerTime annotation to an EndpointSlice\n188: func addTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) {\n189: \tif endpointSlice.Annotations == nil {\n190: \t\tendpointSlice.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n191: \t}\n192: \n193: \tif !triggerTime.IsZero() {\n194: \t\tendpointSlice.Annotations[v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime] = }\n199: \n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\n201: // an EndpointSlice.\n202: func ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) was an update.\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\n217: \tupdated := false\n218: \tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\n219: \tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\n220: \n221: \t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\n222: \t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\n223: \tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\n224: \t\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\n225: \t\t\tcontinue\n226: \t\t}\n227: \t\t// copy endpoint slice labels\n228: \t\tepLabels[key] = value\n229: \t}\n230: \n231: \tfor key, \"label\", value)\n234: \t\t\tcontinue\n235: \t\t}\n236: \t\t// copy service labels\n237: \t\tsvcLabels[key] = value\n238: \t}\n239: \n240: \t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the corresponding service labels\n241: \tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\n242: \t\tupdated = true\n243: \t}\n244: \n245: \t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\n246: \tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\n247: \t\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \"\"\n248: \t} else {\n249: \t\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\n250: \t}\n251: \n252: \t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\n253: \tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\n254: \tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\n255: \n256: \treturn svcLabels, updated\n257: }\n258: \n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\n261: \tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\n262: \t\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\n263: \t\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\n264: \t\treturn true\n265: \t}\n266: \treturn false\n267: }\n268: \n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\n270: // endpoints they contain.\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\n272: \n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\n276: \treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\n277: }\n278: \n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\n281: \tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\n282: \t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\n283: \t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\n284: \t// v1.IPFamily types\n285: \n286: \t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in \t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t}\n297: \n298: \tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\n299: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\n300: \t}\n301: \n302: \t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\n303: \t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\n304: \n305: \t// Why do we need this:\n306: \t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\n307: \t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\n308: \t// if the controller manager connected to old api\n309: \t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\n310: \t// removing all slices already created for this service.\n311: \t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\n312: \t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\n313: \t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\n314: \t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\n315: \t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\n316: \n317: \tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\n318: \t\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\n319: \t\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\n320: \t\t\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\n321: \t\t}\n322: \t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\n323: \t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"addressType\", addrType, \"clusterIP\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\n324: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n325: \t}\n326: \n327: \t// headless\n328: \t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\n329: \t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\n330: \t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\n331: \t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\n332: \tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\n333: \tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n334: \tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service))\n335: \treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n336: }\n337: \n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\n339: \tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\n340: \tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\n341: \t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n342: \t}\n343: \tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\n344: \t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n345: \t}\n346: \tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n347: \tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\n348: \t\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\n349: \t\t\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\n350: \t\t}\n351: \t}\n352: \n353: \treturn unchangedSlices\n354: }\n355: \n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\n358: // with a value set to \"Auto\" or \"auto\". When both are set,\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\n361: \tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\n362: \tif !ok {\n363: \t\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\n364: \t\tif !ok {\n365: \t\t\treturn false\n366: \t\t}\n367: \t}\n368: \treturn val == \"Auto\" || val == \"auto\"\n369: }\n370: \n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\n375: \treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \"\"\n376: }\n377: \n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\n379: // targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\n380: // string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\n381: // match is found, fail.\n382: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n383: func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\n384: \tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\n385: \tswitch portName.Type {\n386: \tcase intstr.String:\n387: \t\tname := portName.StrVal\n388: \t\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n389: \t\t\tfor _, port := range container.Ports {\n390: \t\t\t\tif port.Name == name && port.Protocol == svcPort.Protocol {\n391: \t\t\t\t\treturn int(port.ContainerPort), nil\n392: \t\t\t\t}\n393: \t\t\t}\n394: \t\t}\n395: \tcase intstr.Int:\n396: \t\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\n397: \t}\n398: \n399: \treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\", pod.UID)\n400: }\n401: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/utils.go"}}}

record and publish events.\\n58: \\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\\n59: \\tcontrollerName string\\n60: }\\n61: \\n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\\n63: \\n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\\n67: \\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\\n68: \\t\\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\\n69: \\t}\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group }\\n78: \\n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\\n84: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{} existingSlice.AddressType)\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t\\t}\\n106: \\n107: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n108: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n109: \\t\\t}\\n110: \\n111: make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\\n114: \\t\\t}\\n115: \\n116: \\t\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\\n117: := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\\n123: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n124: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, err)\\n125: \\t\\t}\\n126: \\t}\\n127: \\n128: \\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\\n129: \\t// by the service\\n130: \\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\\n131: \\t\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\n132: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n133: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\\\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\\n134: \\t\\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\\n136: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"delete\\\").Inc()\\n137: \\t\\t}\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, \\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n151: \\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n152: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n153: \\tevents := Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\n198: \\t\\t\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\\n250: \\t\\t\\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it !ok {\n113: \t\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\n114: \t\t}\n115: \n116: \t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\n117: \t}\n118: \n119: \t// reconcile for existing.\n120: \tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\n121: \t\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\n122: \t\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\n123: \t\tif err != nil {\n124: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n125: \t\t}\n126: \t}\n127: \n128: \t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\n129: \t// by the service\n130: \tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\n131: \t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n132: \t\tif err != nil {\n133: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\n134: \t\t} else {\n135: \t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\n136: \t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n137: \t\t}\n138: \t}\n139: \n140: \treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n141: }\n142: \n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. \t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\n235: \t}\n236: \n237: \t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\n238: \t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\n239: \tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\n240: \t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\n241: \t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\n242: \t\t}\n243: \t}\n244: \n245: \t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\n246: \tif len(existingSlices) == \"kube-node-lease\"\n33: )\n34: \n35: // Volume represents a named volume in a pod that may be accessed by any container in the pod.\n36: type Volume struct {\n37: \t// name of the volume.\n38: \t// implied behavior is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.\n44: \tVolumeSource `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=volumeSource\"`\n45: }\n46: \n47: // Represents the source of a volume to mount.\n48: // Only one of its members may be specified.\n49: type VolumeSource struct {\n50: \t// hostPath represents a pre-existing file or directory on the host\n51: \t// machine that is directly exposed to the container. This is generally\n52: \t// used for system agents or other privileged things that are allowed\n53: \t// to see the host machine. \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/helper\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tMODIFIED  = \"modified\"\n\tTAINTED   = \"tainted\"\n\tUNTAINTED = \"untainted\"\n)\n\n// parseTaint parses a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (v1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taint v1.Taint\n\n\tvar key string\n\tvar value string\n\tvar effect v1.TaintEffect\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n\tswitch len(parts) {\n\tcase 1:\n\t\tkey = parts[0]\n\tcase 2:\n\t\teffect = v1.TaintEffect(parts[1])\n\t\tif err := validateTaintEffect(effect); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn taint, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartsKV := strings.Split(parts[0], \"=\")\n\t\tif len(partsKV) > 2 {\n\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey = partsKV[0]\n\t\tif len(partsKV) == 2 {\n\t\t\tvalue = partsKV[1]\n\t\t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t}\n\n\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t}\n\n\ttaint.Key = key\n\ttaint.Value = value\n\ttaint.Effect = effect\n\n\treturn taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ParseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\n// It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\nfunc ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\n\tuniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\n\n\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\n\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\") {\n\t\t\ttaintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\"))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, v1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\n\t\t\tif len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", taintSpec)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\", newTaint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\n\n\t\t\ttaints = append(taints, newTaint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn taints, taintsToRemove, nil\n}\n\n// CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []v1.Taint, taints []v1.Taint) string {\n\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n\t\t\t\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// DeleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc DeleteTaintsByKey(taints []v1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n\tdeleted := false\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n\t\t\tdeleted = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// DeleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc DeleteTaint(taints []v1.Taint, taintToDelete *v1.Taint) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n\tdeleted := false\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\n\t\t\tdeleted = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// RemoveTaint tries to remove a taint from annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc RemoveTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\n\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n\tif len(nodeTaints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif !TaintExists(nodeTaints, taint) {\n\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tnewTaints, _ := DeleteTaint(nodeTaints, taint)\n\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n\treturn newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// AddOrUpdateTaint tries to add a taint to annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc AddOrUpdateTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\n\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n\n\tvar newTaints []v1.Taint\n\tupdated := false\n\tfor i := range nodeTaints {\n\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(&nodeTaints[i]) {\n\t\t\tif helper.Semantic.DeepEqual(*taint, nodeTaints[i]) {\n\t\t\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n\t\t\tupdated = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, nodeTaints[i])\n\t}\n\n\tif !updated {\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n\t}\n\n\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n\treturn newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// TaintExists checks if the given taint exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintToFind *v1.Taint) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(taintToFind) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// TaintKeyExists checks if the given taint key exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintKeyExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintKeyToMatch string) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif taint.Key == taintKeyToMatch {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// TaintSetDiff finds the difference between two taint slices and\n// returns all new and removed elements of the new slice relative to the old slice.\n// for example:\n// input: taintsNew=[a b] taintsOld=[a c]\n// output: taintsToAdd=[b] taintsToRemove=[c]\nfunc TaintSetDiff(taintsNew, taintsOld []v1.Taint) (taintsToAdd []*v1.Taint, taintsToRemove []*v1.Taint) {\n\tfor _, taint := range taintsNew {\n\t\tif !TaintExists(taintsOld, &taint) {\n\t\t\tt := taint\n\t\t\ttaintsToAdd = append(taintsToAdd, &t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, taint := range taintsOld {\n\t\tif !TaintExists(taintsNew, &taint) {\n\t\t\tt := taint\n\t\t\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, &t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn\n}\n\n// TaintSetFilter filters from the taint slice according to the passed fn function to get the filtered taint slice.\nfunc TaintSetFilter(taints []v1.Taint, fn func(*v1.Taint) bool) []v1.Taint {\n\tres := []v1.Taint{}\n\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif fn(&taint) {\n\t\t\tres = append(res, taint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn res\n}\n\n// CheckTaintValidation checks if the given taint is valid.\n// Returns error if the given taint is invalid.\nfunc CheckTaintValidation(taint v1.Taint) error {\n\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(taint.Key); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint key: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t}\n\tif taint.Value != \"\" {\n\t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(taint.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint value: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (corev1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taint corev1.Taint\n\n\tvar key string\n\tvar value string\n\tvar effect corev1.TaintEffect\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n\tswitch len(parts) {\n\tcase 1:\n\t\tkey = parts[0]\n\tcase 2:\n\t\teffect = {\n\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey = partsKV[0]\n\t\tif len(partsKV) == 2 {\n\t\t\tvalue = partsKV[1]\n\t\t\tif errs := \"; \"))\n\t}\n\n\ttaint.Key = key\n\ttaint.Value = value\n\ttaint.Effect = effect\n\n\treturn taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// reorganizeTaints returns the updated set of taints, taking into account old taints that were not updated,\n// old taints that were updated, old taints that were deleted, and new taints.\nfunc reorganizeTaints(node *corev1.Node, overwrite bool, taintsToAdd []corev1.Taint, taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint) (string, []corev1.Taint, error) {\n\tnewTaints := append([]corev1.Taint{}, taintsToAdd...)\n\toldTaints := node.Spec.Taints\n\t// add taints that already existing but not updated to newTaints\n\tadded := addTaints(oldTaints, &newTaints)\n\tallErrs, deleted := deleteTaints(taintsToRemove, &newTaints)\n\tif (added && deleted) || overwrite {\n\t\treturn MODIFIED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n\t} else if added {\n\t\treturn TAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n\t}\n\treturn UNTAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n}\n\n// deleteTaints deletes the given taints from the node's taintlist.\nfunc deleteTaints(taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) ([]error, bool) {\n\tallErrs := []error{}\n\tvar removed bool\n\tfor _, taintToRemove := range taintsToRemove {\n\t\tif taintToRemove.Key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !removed {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, fmt.Errorf(\"taint %q not found\", taintToRemove.ToString()))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs, removed\n}\n\n// addTaints addTaints(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) bool {\n\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\texistsInNew := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range *newTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(&oldTaint) {\n\t\t\t\texistsInNew = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !existsInNew {\n\t\t\t*newTaints = append(*newTaints, oldTaint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn len(oldTaints) != len(*newTaints)\n}\n\n// checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, taints []corev1.Taint) string {\n\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n\t\t\t\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// deleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc deleteTaintsByKey(taints []corev1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]corev1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []corev1.Taint{}\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, len(taints) != len(newTaints)\n}\n\n// deleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc deleteTaint(taints []corev1.Taint, taintToDelete *corev1.Taint) ValidateQualifiedName validates if name is what Kubernetes calls a \"qualified name\".\nfunc ValidateQualifiedName(value string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tfor _, msg := range validation.IsQualifiedName(value) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value, msg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateDNS1123Subdomain validates that a name is a proper DNS subdomain.\nfunc ValidateDNS1123Subdomain(value string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tfor _, msg := range validation.IsDNS1123Subdomain(value) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value, msg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc ValidatePodSpecificAnnotations(annotations map[string]string, spec *core.PodSpec, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tif value, isMirror := annotations[core.MirrorPodAnnotationKey]; isMirror {\n\t\tif len(spec.NodeName) == 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Key(core.MirrorPodAnnotationKey), value, \"must set spec.nodeName if mirror pod annotation is set\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif annotations[core.TolerationsAnnotationKey] != \"\" {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateTolerationsInPodAnnotations(annotations, fldPath)...)\n\t}\n\n\tif !opts.AllowInvalidPodDeletionCost {\n\t\tif _, err := helper.GetDeletionCostFromPodAnnotations(annotations); err != nil {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Key(core.PodDeletionCost), annotations[core.PodDeletionCost], \"must be a 32bit integer\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateSeccompPodAnnotations(annotations, fldPath)...)\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateAppArmorPodAnnotations(annotations, spec, fldPath)...)\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateTolerationsInPodAnnotations tests that the serialized tolerations in Pod.Annotations has valid data\nfunc ValidateTolerationsInPodAnnotations(annotations map[string]string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\ttolerations, err := helper.GetTolerationsFromPodAnnotations(annotations)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, core.TolerationsAnnotationKey, err.Error()))\n\t\treturn allErrs\n\t}\n\n\tif len(tolerations) > 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateTolerations(tolerations, fldPath.Child(core.TolerationsAnnotationKey))...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc ValidatePodSpecificAnnotationUpdates(newPod, oldPod *core.Pod, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tnewAnnotations := newPod.Annotations\n\toldAnnotations := oldPod.Annotations\n\tfor k, oldVal := range oldAnnotations {\n\t\tif newVal, exists := newAnnotations[k]; exists && newVal == oldVal {\n\t\t\tcontinue // No change.\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, v1.DeprecatedAppArmorBetaContainerAnnotationKeyPrefix) {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Key(k), \"may not remove or update AppArmor annotations\"))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif k == core.MirrorPodAnnotationKey {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Key(k), \"may not remove or update mirror pod annotation\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Check for additions\n\tfor k := range newAnnotations {\n\t\tif _, ok := oldAnnotations[k]; ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue // No change.\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, v1.DeprecatedAppArmorBetaContainerAnnotationKeyPrefix) {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Key(k), \"may not add AppArmor annotations\"))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif k == core.MirrorPodAnnotationKey {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Key(k), \"may not add mirror pod annotation\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidatePodSpecificAnnotations(newAnnotations, &newPod.Spec, fldPath, opts)...)\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc ValidateEndpointsSpecificAnnotations(annotations map[string]string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateNameFunc validates that the provided name is valid for a given resource type.\n// Not all resources have the same validation rules for names. Prefix is true\n// if the name will have a value appended to it.  If the name is not valid,\n// this returns a list of descriptions of individual characteristics of the\n// value that were not valid.  Otherwise this returns an empty list or nil.\ntype ValidateNameFunc apimachineryvalidation.ValidateNameFunc\n\n// ValidatePodName can be used to check whether the given pod name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidatePodName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateReplicationControllerName can be used to check whether the given replication\n// controller name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateReplicationControllerName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateServiceName can be used to check whether the given service name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateServiceName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNS1035Label\n\n// ValidateNodeName can be used to check whether the given node name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateNodeName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateNamespaceName can be used to check whether the given namespace name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateNamespaceName = apimachineryvalidation.ValidateNamespaceName\n\n// ValidateLimitRangeName can be used to check whether the given limit range name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateLimitRangeName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateResourceQuotaName can be used to check whether the given\n// resource quota name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateResourceQuotaName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateSecretName can be used to check whether the given secret name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateSecretName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateServiceAccountName can be used to check whether the given service account name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateServiceAccountName = apimachineryvalidation.ValidateServiceAccountName\n\n// ValidateEndpointsName can be used to check whether the given endpoints name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nvar ValidateEndpointsName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateClassName can be used to check whether the given class name is valid.\n// It is defined here to avoid import cycle between pkg/apis/storage/validation\n// (where it should be) and this file.\nvar ValidateClassName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidatePriorityClassName can be used to check whether the given priority\n// class name is valid.\nvar ValidatePriorityClassName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateResourceClaimName can be used to check whether the given\n// name for a ResourceClaim is valid.\nvar ValidateResourceClaimName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateResourceClaimTemplateName can be used to check whether the given\n// name for a ResourceClaimTemplate is valid.\nvar ValidateResourceClaimTemplateName = apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain\n\n// ValidateRuntimeClassName can be used to check whether the given RuntimeClass name is valid.\n// Prefix indicates this name will be used as part of generation, in which case\n// trailing dashes are allowed.\nfunc ValidateRuntimeClassName(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n\tfor _, msg := range apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain(name, false) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, name, msg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// validateOverhead can be used to check whether the given Overhead is valid.\nfunc validateOverhead(overhead core.ResourceList, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\t// reuse the ResourceRequirements validation logic\n\treturn ValidateResourceRequirements(&core.ResourceRequirements{Limits: overhead}, nil, fldPath, opts)\n}\n\n// Validates that given value is not negative.\nfunc ValidateNonnegativeField(value int64, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\treturn apimachineryvalidation.ValidateNonnegativeField(value, fldPath)\n}\n\n// Validates that a Quantity is not negative\nfunc ValidateNonnegativeQuantity(value resource.Quantity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif value.Cmp(resource.Quantity{}) < 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value.String(), isNegativeErrorMsg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// Validates that a Quantity is positive\nfunc ValidatePositiveQuantityValue(value resource.Quantity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif value.Cmp(resource.Quantity{}) <= 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value.String(), isNotPositiveErrorMsg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc ValidateImmutableField(newVal, oldVal interface{}, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\treturn apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(newVal, oldVal, fldPath)\n}\n\nfunc ValidateImmutableAnnotation(newVal string, oldVal string, annotation string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tif oldVal != newVal {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"annotations\", annotation), newVal, fieldImmutableErrorMsg))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateObjectMeta validates an object's metadata on creation. It expects that name generation has already\n// been performed.\n// It doesn't return an error for rootscoped resources with namespace, because namespace should already be cleared before.\n// TODO: Remove calls to this method scattered in validations of specific resources, e.g., ValidatePodUpdate.\nfunc ValidateObjectMeta(meta *metav1.ObjectMeta, requiresNamespace bool, nameFn ValidateNameFunc, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := apimachineryvalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(meta, requiresNamespace, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateNameFunc(nameFn), fldPath)\n\t// run additional checks for the finalizer name\n\tfor i := range meta.Finalizers {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateKubeFinalizerName(string(meta.Finalizers[i]), fldPath.Child(\"finalizers\").Index(i))...)\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateObjectMetaUpdate validates an object's metadata when updated\nfunc ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(newMeta, oldMeta *metav1.ObjectMeta, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := apimachineryvalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(newMeta, oldMeta, fldPath)\n\t// run additional checks for the finalizer name\n\tfor i := range newMeta.Finalizers {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateKubeFinalizerName(string(newMeta.Finalizers[i]), fldPath.Child(\"finalizers\").Index(i))...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc ValidateVolumes(volumes []core.Volume, podMeta *metav1.ObjectMeta, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) (map[string]core.VolumeSource, field.ErrorList) {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tallNames := sets.Set[string]{}\n\tallCreatedPVCs := sets.Set[string]{}\n\t// Determine which PVCs will be created for this pod. We need\n\t// the exact name of the pod for this. Without it, this sanity\n\t// check has to be skipped.\n\tif podMeta != nil && podMeta.Name != \"\" {\n\t\tfor _, vol := range volumes {\n\t\t\tif vol.VolumeSource.Ephemeral != nil {\n\t\t\t\tallCreatedPVCs.Insert(podMeta.Name + \"-\" + vol.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tvols := make(map[string]core.VolumeSource)\n\tfor i, vol := range volumes {\n\t\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\n\t\tnamePath := idxPath.Child(\"name\")\n\t\tel := validateVolumeSource(&vol.VolumeSource, idxPath, vol.Name, podMeta, opts)\n\t\tif len(vol.Name) == 0 {\n\t\t\tel = append(el, field.Required(namePath, \"\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tel = append(el, ValidateDNS1123Label(vol.Name, namePath)...)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif allNames.Has(vol.Name) {\n\t\t\tel = append(el, field.Duplicate(namePath, vol.Name))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(el) == 0 {\n\t\t\tallNames.Insert(vol.Name)\n\t\t\tvols[vol.Name] = vol.VolumeSource\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, el...)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// A PersistentVolumeClaimSource should not reference a created PVC. That doesn't\n\t\t// make sense.\n\t\tif vol.PersistentVolumeClaim != nil && allCreatedPVCs.Has(vol.PersistentVolumeClaim.ClaimName) {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(idxPath.Child(\"persistentVolumeClaim\").Child(\"claimName\"), vol.PersistentVolumeClaim.ClaimName,\n\t\t\t\t\"must not reference a PVC that gets created for an ephemeral volume\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn vols, allErrs\n}\n\nfunc IsMatchedVolume(name string, volumes map[string]core.VolumeSource) bool {\n\tif _, ok := volumes[name]; ok {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// isMatched checks whether the volume with the given name is used by a\n// container and if so, if it involves a PVC.\nfunc isMatchedDevice(name string, volumes map[string]core.VolumeSource) (isMatched bool, isPVC bool) {\n\tif source, ok := volumes[name]; ok {\n\t\tif source.PersistentVolumeClaim != nil ||\n\t\t\tsource.Ephemeral != nil {\n\t\t\treturn true, true\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn true, false\n\t}\n\treturn false, false\n}\n\nfunc mountNameAlreadyExists(name string, devices map[string]string) bool {\n\tif _, ok := devices[name]; ok {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc mountPathAlreadyExists(mountPath string, devices map[string]string) bool {\n\tfor _, devPath := range devices {\n\t\tif mountPath == devPath {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc deviceNameAlreadyExists(name string, mounts map[string]string) bool {\n\tif _, ok := mounts[name]; ok {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc devicePathAlreadyExists(devicePath string, mounts map[string]string) bool {\n\tfor _, mountPath := range mounts {\n\t\tif mountPath == devicePath {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc validateVolumeSource(source *core.VolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path, volName string, podMeta *metav1.ObjectMeta, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tnumVolumes := validatePhotonPersistentDiskVolumeSource(source.PhotonPersistentDisk, fldPath.Child(\"photonPersistentDisk\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.PortworxVolume != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"portworxVolume\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePortworxVolumeSource(source.PortworxVolume, fldPath.Child(\"portworxVolume\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.AzureDisk != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, fldPath.Child(\"azureDisk\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.StorageOS != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"storageos\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateStorageOSVolumeSource(source.StorageOS, fldPath.Child(\"storageos\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.Projected != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"projected\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateProjectedVolumeSource(source.Projected, fldPath.Child(\"projected\"), opts)...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.ScaleIO != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"scaleIO\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateScaleIOVolumeSource(source.ScaleIO, fldPath.Child(\"scaleIO\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.CSI != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"csi\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateCSIVolumeSource(source.CSI, fldPath.Child(\"csi\"))...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif source.Ephemeral != nil {\n\t\tif numVolumes > 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"ephemeral\"), \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnumVolumes++\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateEphemeralVolumeSource(source.Ephemeral, fldPath.Child(\"ephemeral\"))...)\n\t\t\t// Check the expected name for the PVC. This gets skipped if information is missing,\n\t\t\t// because that already gets flagged as a problem elsewhere. For example,\n\t\t\t// ValidateObjectMeta as called by validatePodMetadataAndSpec checks that the name is set.\n\t\t\tif podMeta != nil && podMeta.Name != \"\" && volName != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tpvcName := podMeta.Name + \"-\" + volName\n\t\t\t\tfor _, msg := range ValidatePersistentVolumeName(pvcName, false) {\n\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"name\"), volName, fmt.Sprintf(\"PVC name %q: %v\", pvcName, msg)))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif numVolumes == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"must specify a volume type\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateHostPathVolumeSource(hostPath *core.HostPathVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif \"\"))\n\t} else {\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(iscsi.IQN, \"iqn\") && !strings.HasPrefix(iscsi.IQN, \"eui\") && !strings.HasPrefix(iscsi.IQN, \"naa\") {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, \"iqn\") && !iscsiInitiatorIqnRegex.MatchString(initiator) {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"initiatorname\"), initiator, \"must be valid format\"))\n\t\t} else if sets.New(\n\t\"metadata.name\",\n\t\"metadata.namespace\",\n\t\"metadata.labels\",\n\t\"metadata.annotations\",\n\t\"metadata.uid\")\n\nfunc validateDownwardAPIVolumeFile(file *core.DownwardAPIVolumeFile, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(file.Path) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validContainerResourceFieldPathPrefixesWithDownwardAPIHugePages\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateContainerResourceFieldSelector(file.ResourceFieldRef, &validContainerResourceFieldPathExpressions, &localValidContainerResourceFieldPathPrefixes, fldPath.Child(\"resourceFieldRef\"), true)...)\n\t} else {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tdownwardAPIMode := downwardAPIVolume.DefaultMode\n\tif downwardAPIMode != nil && (*downwardAPIMode > 0777 || *downwardAPIMode < 0) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"defaultMode\"), *downwardAPIMode, fileModeErrorMsg))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, file := range downwardAPIVolume.Items {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDownwardAPIVolumeFile(&file, fldPath, opts)...)\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateProjectionSources(projection *core.ProjectedVolumeSource, projectionMode *int32, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tallPaths := sets.Set[string]{}\n\n\tfor i, source := range projection.Sources {\n\t\tnumSources := 0\n\t\tsrcPath := fldPath.Child(\"sources\").Index(i)\n\t\tif projPath := srcPath.Child(\"secret\"); source.Secret != nil {\n\t\t\tnumSources++\n\t\t\tif len(source.Secret.Name) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath.Child(\"name\"), \"\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\titemsPath := projPath.Child(\"items\")\n\t\t\tfor i, kp := range source.Secret.Items {\n\t\t\t\titemPath := itemsPath.Index(i)\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateKeyToPath(&kp, itemPath)...)\n\t\t\t\tif len(kp.Path) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tcurPath := kp.Path\n\t\t\t\t\tif !allPaths.Has(curPath) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallPaths.Insert(curPath)\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, source.Secret.Name, \"conflicting duplicate paths\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif projPath := srcPath.Child(\"configMap\"); source.ConfigMap != nil {\n\t\t\tnumSources++\n\t\t\tif len(source.ConfigMap.Name) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath.Child(\"name\"), \"\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\titemsPath := projPath.Child(\"items\")\n\t\t\tfor i, kp := range source.ConfigMap.Items {\n\t\t\t\titemPath := itemsPath.Index(i)\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateKeyToPath(&kp, itemPath)...)\n\t\t\t\tif len(kp.Path) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tcurPath := kp.Path\n\t\t\t\t\tif !allPaths.Has(curPath) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallPaths.Insert(curPath)\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, source.ConfigMap.Name, \"conflicting duplicate paths\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif projPath := srcPath.Child(\"downwardAPI\"); source.DownwardAPI != nil {\n\t\t\tnumSources++\n\t\t\tfor _, file := range source.DownwardAPI.Items {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDownwardAPIVolumeFile(&file, projPath, opts)...)\n\t\t\t\tif len(file.Path) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tcurPath := file.Path\n\t\t\t\t\tif !allPaths.Has(curPath) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallPaths.Insert(curPath)\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, curPath, \"conflicting duplicate paths\"))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif projPath := srcPath.Child(\"serviceAccountToken\"); source.ServiceAccountToken != nil {\n\t\t\tnumSources++\n\t\t\tif source.ServiceAccountToken.ExpirationSeconds < 10*60 {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath.Child(\"expirationSeconds\"), source.ServiceAccountToken.ExpirationSeconds, \"may not specify a duration less than 10 minutes\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif source.ServiceAccountToken.ExpirationSeconds > 1<<32 {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath.Child(\"expirationSeconds\"), source.ServiceAccountToken.ExpirationSeconds, \"may not specify a duration larger than 2^32 seconds\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif source.ServiceAccountToken.Path == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"path\"), \"\"))\n\t\t\t} else if !opts.AllowNonLocalProjectedTokenPath {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateLocalNonReservedPath(source.ServiceAccountToken.Path, fldPath.Child(\"path\"))...)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif projPath := srcPath.Child(\"clusterTrustBundlePEM\"); source.ClusterTrustBundle != nil {\n\t\t\tnumSources++\n\n\t\t\tusingName := source.ClusterTrustBundle.Name != nil\n\t\t\tusingSignerName := source.ClusterTrustBundle.SignerName != nil\n\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase usingName && usingSignerName:\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath, source.ClusterTrustBundle, \"only one of name and signerName may be used\"))\n\t\t\tcase usingName:\n\t\t\t\tif *source.ClusterTrustBundle.Name == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath.Child(\"name\"), \"must be a valid object name\"))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tname := *source.ClusterTrustBundle.Name\n\t\t\t\tif signerName, ok := extractSignerNameFromClusterTrustBundleName(name); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalidationFunc := ValidateClusterTrustBundleName(signerName)\n\t\t\t\t\terrMsgs := validationFunc(name, false)\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, msg := range errMsgs {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath.Child(\"name\"), name, fmt.Sprintf(\"not a valid clustertrustbundlename: %v\", msg)))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tvalidationFunc := ValidateClusterTrustBundleName(\"\")\n\t\t\t\t\terrMsgs := validationFunc(name, false)\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, msg := range errMsgs {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath.Child(\"name\"), name, fmt.Sprintf(\"not a valid clustertrustbundlename: %v\", msg)))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif source.ClusterTrustBundle.LabelSelector != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(projPath.Child(\"labelSelector\"), source.ClusterTrustBundle.LabelSelector, \"labelSelector must be unset if name is specified\"))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase usingSignerName:\n\t\t\t\tif *source.ClusterTrustBundle.SignerName == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath.Child(\"signerName\"), \"must be a valid signer name\"))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateSignerName(projPath.Child(\"signerName\"), *source.ClusterTrustBundle.SignerName)...)\n\n\t\t\t\tlabelSelectorErrs := unversionedvalidation.ValidateLabelSelector(\n\t\t\t\t\tsource.ClusterTrustBundle.LabelSelector,\n\t\t\t\t\tunversionedvalidation.LabelSelectorValidationOptions{AllowInvalidLabelValueInSelector: false},\n\t\t\t\t\tprojPath.Child(\"labelSelector\"),\n\t\t\t\t)\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, labelSelectorErrs...)\n\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath, \"either name or signerName must be specified\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif source.ClusterTrustBundle.Path == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(projPath.Child(\"path\"), \"\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, ValidateLocalNonReservedPath(source.ClusterTrustBundle.Path, projPath.Child(\"path\"))...)\n\n\t\t\tcurPath := source.ClusterTrustBundle.Path\n\t\t\tif !allPaths.Has(curPath) {\n\t\t\t\tallPaths.Insert(curPath)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, curPath, \"conflicting duplicate paths\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif numSources > 1 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(srcPath, \"may not specify more than 1 volume type\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateProjectedVolumeSource(projection *core.ProjectedVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path, opts PodValidationOptions) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tprojectionMode := projection.DefaultMode\n\tif projectionMode != nil && (*projectionMode > 0777 || *projectionMode < 0) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"defaultMode\"), *projectionMode, fileModeErrorMsg))\n\t}\n\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateProjectionSources(projection, projectionMode, fldPath, opts)...)\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nvar supportedHostPathTypes = sets.New(\n\tcore.HostPathUnset,\n\tcore.HostPathDirectoryOrCreate,\n\tcore.HostPathDirectory,\n\tcore.HostPathFileOrCreate,\n\tcore.HostPathFile,\n\tcore.HostPathSocket,\n\tcore.HostPathCharDev,\n\tcore.HostPathBlockDev)\n\nfunc validateHostPathType(hostPathType *core.HostPathType, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tif hostPathType != nil && !supportedHostPathTypes.Has(*hostPathType) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath, hostPathType, sets.List(supportedHostPathTypes)))\n\t}\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// This validate will make sure targetPath:\n// 1. is not abs path\n// 2. does not have any element which is \"..\"\nfunc validateLocalDescendingPath(targetPath string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif path.IsAbs(targetPath) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, targetPath, \"must be a relative path\"))\n\t}\n\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePathNoBacksteps(targetPath, fldPath)...)\n\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// validatePathNoBacksteps makes sure the targetPath does not have any `..` path elements when split\n//\n// This assumes the OS of the apiserver and the nodes are the same. The same check should be done\n// on the node to ensure there are no backsteps.\nfunc validatePathNoBacksteps(targetPath string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tparts := strings.Split(filepath.ToSlash(targetPath), \"/\")\n\tfor _, item := range parts {\n\t\tif item == \"..\" {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, targetPath, \"must not contain '..'\"))\n\t\t\tbreak // even for `../../..`, one error is sufficient to make the point\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// validateMountPropagation verifies that MountPropagation field is valid and\n// allowed for given container.\nfunc validateMountPropagation(mountPropagation *core.MountPropagationMode, container *core.Container, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\n\tif mountPropagation == nil {\n\t\treturn allErrs\n\t}\n\n\tsupportedMountPropagations := sets.New(\n\t\tcore.MountPropagationBidirectional,\n\t\tcore.MountPropagationHostToContainer,\n\t\tcore.MountPropagationNone)\n\n\tif !supportedMountPropagations.Has(*mountPropagation) {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *mountPropagation, sets.List(supportedMountPropagations)))\n\t}\n\n\tif container == nil {\n\t\t// The container is not available yet.\n\t\t// Stop validation now, Pod validation will refuse final\n\t\t// Pods with Bidirectional propagation in non-privileged containers.\n\t\treturn allErrs\n\t}\n\n\tprivileged := container.SecurityContext != nil && container.SecurityContext.Privileged != nil && *container.SecurityContext.Privileged\n\tif *mountPropagation == core.MountPropagationBidirectional && !privileged {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath, \"Bidirectional mount propagation is available only to privileged containers\"))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// validateMountRecursiveReadOnly validates RecursiveReadOnly mounts.\nfunc validateMountRecursiveReadOnly(mount core.VolumeMount, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tif mount.RecursiveReadOnly == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tswitch *mount.RecursiveReadOnly {\n\tcase core.RecursiveReadOnlyDisabled:\n\t\t// NOP\n\tcase core.RecursiveReadOnlyEnabled, core.RecursiveReadOnlyIfPossible:\n\t\tif !mount.ReadOnly {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath, \"may only be specified when readOnly is true\"))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif mount.MountPropagation != nil && *mount.MountPropagation != core.MountPropagationNone {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath, \"may only be specified when mountPropagation is None or not specified\"))\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\tsupportedRRO := sets.New(\n\t\t\tcore.RecursiveReadOnlyDisabled,\n\t\t\tcore.RecursiveReadOnlyIfPossible,\n\t\t\tcore.RecursiveReadOnlyEnabled)\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *mount.RecursiveReadOnly, sets.List(supportedRRO)))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\n// ValidateLocalNonReservedPath makes sure targetPath:\n// 1. is not abs path\n// 2. does not contain any '..' elements\n// 3. does not start with '..'\nfunc ValidateLocalNonReservedPath(targetPath string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateLocalDescendingPath(targetPath, fldPath)...)\n\t// Don't report this error if the check for .. elements already caught it.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(targetPath, \"..\") && !strings.HasPrefix(targetPath, \"../\") {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, targetPath, \"must not start with '..'\"))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateRBDVolumeSource(rbd *core.RBDVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(rbd.CephMonitors) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"monitors\"), \"\"))\n\t}\n\tif len(rbd.RBDImage) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"image\"), \"\"))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateRBDPersistentVolumeSource(rbd *core.RBDPersistentVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(rbd.CephMonitors) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateCinderVolumeSource(cd *core.CinderVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(cd.VolumeID) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"volumeID\"), \"\"))\n\t}\n\tif cd.SecretRef != nil {\n\t\tif len(cd.SecretRef.Name) == 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"secretRef\", \"name\"), \"\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateCinderPersistentVolumeSource(cd *core.CinderPersistentVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(cd.VolumeID) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"volumeID\"), \"\"))\n\t}\n\tif cd.SecretRef != nil {\n\t\tif len(cd.SecretRef.Name) == 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"secretRef\", \"name\"), \"\"))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(cd.SecretRef.Namespace) == 0 {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"secretRef\", \"namespace\"), \"\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs\n}\n\nfunc validateCephFSVolumeSource(cephfs *core.CephFSVolumeSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n\tif len(cephfs.Monitors) == 0 {\n\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"monitors\"), \"\"))\n\t}\n\treturn
